gaps.Title: GOV-001
gaps.field_3: Management body approves cybersecurity risk management measures (Art.21)
gaps.field_7: Board resolution document approving all Art.21 measures

gaps.Title: GOV-002
gaps.field_3: Management body oversees implementation of Art.21 measures
gaps.field_4: ⚠️ CISO reports to board ad-hoc only
gaps.field_7: Quarterly cybersecurity board agenda item with minutes

gaps.Title: GOV-003
gaps.field_3: Management body can be held liable for Art.21 infringements
gaps.field_4: ❌ Personal liability not communicated to board
gaps.field_7: Legal briefing delivered to board; signed acknowledgement

gaps.Title: GOV-004
gaps.field_3: Management body completes mandatory cybersecurity training
gaps.field_7: Training completion certificates for all board members

gaps.Title: GOV-005
gaps.field_3: Management encourages employees to complete regular security training
gaps.field_4: ⚠️ Annual training exists but not board-driven
gaps.field_7: Board-endorsed training mandate communicated to all staff

gaps.Title: GOV-006
gaps.field_3: Senior management has sufficient knowledge to identify risks
gaps.field_7: Cyber risk literacy assessment for C-suite

gaps.Title: GOV-007
gaps.field_3: Organisation registers with national authority (ACN) if required
gaps.field_4: ⚠️ Registration status with ACN not confirmed
gaps.field_7: ACN registration confirmation / LIST SpA entity registration

gaps.Title: GOV-008
gaps.field_3: Cybersecurity governance policy documented and approved
gaps.field_7: Standalone Board Cybersecurity Governance Policy document

gaps.Title: GAP-001
gaps.field_3: Risk Analysis & Information Security Policies
gaps.field_4: ISO 27001 ISMS documented; annual risk assessment performed. DORA ICT Risk Framework aligned.
gaps.field_5: Risk assessment does not explicitly reference NIS2 / D.Lgs.138/2024. No documented threat-intelligence framework feeding the risk register. Risk language not aligned between IT and business stakeholders. Risk register lacks Inherent Risk Score formula.
gaps.field_10:
1. Update risk assessment template to include NIS2 Art.21.2.a and D.Lgs.138/2024 mapping column.
2. Introduce Threat Intelligence feed (OSINT + commercial) to update risk register quarterly.
3. Add Inherent Risk Score formula (Likelihood × Impact) to Risk Register tab.
4. Develop unified risk language glossary for board and technical teams.
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.6 | ISO: A.5, A.6 | Priority Score: 7

gaps.Title: GAP-002
gaps.field_3: Incident Handling (24h/72h/1-month Reporting)
gaps.field_4: IR Plan v1.3 exists. ACN/CSIRT notification partially documented. No dedicated 24h Early Warning template.
gaps.field_5: CRITICAL LEGAL DEADLINE. No formal 24h Early Warning template exists. No documented on-call escalation tree for after-hours incidents. No integration with ACN unified digital portal tested. DORA parallel notification to Bank of Italy not procedurally linked. No automated incident detection → escalation trigger.
gaps.field_10:
1. CREATE 24h Early Warning template (ACT-001) by Mar 2026 – URGENT.
2. Define and publish on-call escalation tree with personal contact details.
3. Test ACN portal submission with a tabletop exercise.
4. Link DORA Art.19 Bank of Italy notification into the IR playbook.
5. Configure SIEM (Splunk) alert rule to auto-trigger war-room notification.
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.19 | ISO: A.16 | Priority Score: 10

gaps.Title: GAP-003
gaps.field_3: Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery
gaps.field_4: BC/DR Plan reviewed annually. Immutable backups deployed post-ION LockBit 2023. RTO/RPO defined.
gaps.field_5: BC/DR plan is in place but last test results not formally documented. Backup immutability certificates not verified since Jan 2026. No BIA (Business Impact Analysis) linked to NIS2 classification of critical services. No formal crisis communications protocol with ACN during a prolonged outage.
gaps.field_10:
1. Document annual BC test results and attach to this workbook.
2. Verify Veeam immutability certificates and record expiry dates in Asset Inventory.
3. Conduct BIA mapping critical services (AST-001, AST-003) to NIS2 continuity obligations.
4. Draft crisis communications template for ACN notification during extended downtime.
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.11 | ISO: A.17 | Priority Score: 4

gaps.Title: GAP-004
gaps.field_3: Supply Chain Security (incl. PSNC/CVCN 45-day rule)
gaps.field_4: Vendor list in procurement. No formal NIS2/DORA vetting questionnaire. CVCN not tracked for all strategic suppliers.
gaps.field_5: ION Cloud Infrastructure (AST-002) CVCN notice issued but NOT yet approved – 45-day PSNC rule at risk of breach (criminal offence). Splunk and Veeam questionnaires NOT sent. No tiered vendor risk methodology. No contractual NIS2/DORA security clauses in existing contracts. Cleaning/physical access contractors not assessed.
gaps.field_10:
1. URGENT: Chase CVCN approval for ION Cloud (AST-002) – track daily (ACT-005).
2. Send NIS2 questionnaire to Splunk (VND-004) and Veeam (VND-006) immediately.
3. Define 3-tier vendor risk framework (Critical/Important/Ordinary) with scoring matrix.
4. Insert NIS2/DORA security clauses into all Critical/Important vendor renewals.
5. Assess cleaning contractors and facility access providers for physical security.
6. Build ACN Cloud Catalog check into all new SaaS onboarding (ACT-010).
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.28 | ISO: A.15 | Priority Score: 9

gaps.Title: GAP-005
gaps.field_3: Security in Network / System Acquisition, Development & Maintenance
gaps.field_4: SSDLC policy exists. SAST/DAST in CI/CD pipeline. Secure coding training completed 2025.
gaps.field_5: SSDLC policy exists but not formally mapped to NIS2 / DORA requirements. No mandatory penetration testing gate before major releases. Threat modelling not embedded in design phase. Third-party library (SCA) scanning not formally documented. No formal vulnerability disclosure policy published.
gaps.field_10:
1. Update SSDLC documentation to reference NIS2 Art.21.2.e and DORA Art.9.
2. Add mandatory pen-test sign-off gate in release pipeline for trading platform changes.
3. Embed STRIDE threat modelling in sprint planning for all critical-asset projects.
4. Formalise SCA (Software Composition Analysis) scanning and document results.
5. Publish Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP) on corporate website.
gaps.field_17: DORA: Partial | ISO: A.14 | Priority Score: 6

gaps.Title: GAP-006
gaps.field_3: Effectiveness Assessment – Penetration Testing / TLPT
gaps.field_4: Annual external pen test conducted. No DORA TLPT performed yet.
gaps.field_5: DORA mandates Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) by an ACN-accredited provider – NOT yet scheduled. Annual pen test scope does not formally cover OT/trading infrastructure. No documented criteria for selecting pen test scope aligned with threat intelligence. Previous pen test findings remediation tracking is informal.
gaps.field_10:
1. Select ACN-accredited TLPT provider and submit to ACN for approval (ACT-004).
2. Expand annual pen test scope to explicitly include trading infrastructure (AST-001, AST-003).
3. Define TLPT scope based on Threat Intelligence findings (RSK-001, RSK-002).
4. Create formal pen test finding tracker linked to Remediation Roadmap.
5. Document remediation of all Critical/High findings within 90 days.
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.26 | ISO: A.18 | Priority Score: 8

gaps.Title: GAP-007
gaps.field_3: Cyber Hygiene & Training (incl. Board Personal Liability – NIS2 Art.20)
gaps.field_4: Annual security awareness training for all staff. Board members have NOT completed NIS2 training.
gaps.field_5: CRITICAL: NIS2 Art.20 makes board/management personally liable. Board training completion = 0%. No NIS2-specific training module exists. Phishing simulation conducted but results not formally documented. No gamified or role-based training program. Training records not systematically maintained for audit evidence.
gaps.field_10:
1. URGENT: Schedule Board NIS2 briefing by Mar 2026 (ACT-002) – personal liability applies.
2. Create NIS2-specific training module covering Art.20/21 obligations.
3. Implement role-based training tracks: Board, IT/Security, General Staff.
4. Document all training completions in HR system for compliance evidence.
5. Run quarterly phishing simulations and formally log results.
6. Introduce gamified security awareness platform.
gaps.field_17: DORA: Partial | ISO: A.7 | Priority Score: 10

gaps.Title: GAP-008
gaps.field_3: Cryptography & Encryption
gaps.field_4: AES-256 at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit. FIPS 140-2 validated modules in use. ACN standards cross-check pending.
gaps.field_5: ACN-approved cryptographic standards not yet cross-checked for 'Strategic' data assets. Key management lifecycle (rotation schedule, HSM usage, expiry monitoring) not formally documented. Trading desk voice/text communications encryption not confirmed. Cryptographic incident-handling policy not tested.
gaps.field_10:
1. Cross-check all cryptographic implementations against ACN D.Lgs.138/2024 approved standards (ACT-009).
2. Document key management policy including rotation schedule and HSM usage.
3. Confirm and document trading desk voice/text encryption status.
4. Test cryptographic incident-handling procedure (compromised key scenario).
5. Add crypto standard compliance column to Asset Inventory for Strategic assets.
gaps.field_17: DORA: Partial | ISO: A.10 | Priority Score: 5

gaps.Title: GAP-009
gaps.field_3: Human Resources Security & Access Control
gaps.field_4: Background checks pre-hire. PAM (CyberArk) deployed. Quarterly access reviews in place.
gaps.field_5: Privileged access revocation on employee exit not confirmed to occur within 24h. PAM audit trail not yet extended to NIS2 evidence format. Job role change authorisation process informal. Access rights for contractors and third-party suppliers not systematically reviewed. No insider threat detection programme.
gaps.field_10:
1. Implement automated 24h privileged access revocation on HR system termination trigger.
2. Export quarterly PAM reports in NIS2 audit-ready format.
3. Formalise job-role change access authorisation workflow with documented approvals.
4. Extend quarterly access reviews to include contractor and third-party accounts.
5. Configure SIEM (Splunk/AST-005) insider threat use-case alert rules.
gaps.field_17: DORA: Partial | ISO: A.7, A.9 | Priority Score: 5

gaps.Title: GAP-010
gaps.field_3: MFA, Access Control & Secure Communications
gaps.field_4: MFA enforced via Azure AD for all admins and remote access. Trading desk encryption under review.
gaps.field_5: MFA not yet enforced for ALL internal SaaS tools – gap in zero-trust posture. Trading desk voice/text communications encryption status unconfirmed. No formal register of all applications with MFA status. Privileged session recording not confirmed for all admin activities.
gaps.field_10:
1. Deploy MFA to all remaining internal SaaS applications (ACT-007) – map all apps.
2. Confirm and document trading desk secure communications solution.
3. Create MFA coverage register with application owner and compliance status.
4. Enable privileged session recording in CyberArk for all admin activities.
5. Implement Conditional Access policies in Azure AD based on risk score.
gaps.field_17: DORA: DORA Art.9 | ISO: A.9 | Priority Score: 6

gaps.Title: MG-001
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.20 – Governance
gaps.field_5: The framework documents management training as a gap (ACT-002) but does NOT include an Art.20 Governance policy, board approval records, or evidence of board oversight of cybersecurity risk management measures.
gaps.field_10:
1. Draft a Board Cybersecurity Governance Policy.
2. Create a board resolution template for approving Article 21 measures.
3. Add a 'Board Oversight' log to track cybersecurity agenda items.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: CRITICAL – Board members personally face fines, prosecution, suspension from managerial roles

gaps.Title: MG-002
gaps.field_2: NIS2 Art.21.2.a / KPMG & Christopher Nett guidance
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.a – Threat Intelligence
gaps.field_5: The risk register lists threats but there is no documented CTI process (threat actor profiling, TTP mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, IOC feeds). NIS2 requires a risk analysis that identifies and documents key threats systematically.
gaps.field_10:
1. Subscribe to financial sector ISAC (FS-ISAC) for threat intelligence.
2. Map top threat actors (e.g., APT28, Lazarus Group) to LIST SpA's asset profile.
3. Integrate threat intelligence into quarterly risk register reviews.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – Threats not identified before exploitation; reactive rather than proactive security

gaps.Title: MG-003
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.a – Asset Classification
gaps.field_5: Asset Inventory uses 'Strategic/Critical/Ordinary/Important' labels but there is no formal data classification policy defining these tiers, their handling requirements, or their link to NIS2 primary/support asset concept.
gaps.field_10:
1. Draft a Data Classification Policy with NIS2-aligned tiers.
2. Map each Asset Inventory entry to a primary/support asset category.
3. Define handling procedures per classification tier.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: MEDIUM – Incorrect handling of sensitive data could trigger regulatory breach

gaps.Title: MG-004
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.b – Incident Response Plan Gaps
gaps.field_5: The IR checklist exists but there is no document defining SOC team roles, shift coverage, alert escalation SLAs (e.g., P1 alert must reach CISO within 15 min), or a named 24/7 on-call roster. NIS2's 24h deadline makes this legally critical.
gaps.field_10:
1. Create an IR Team Charter with named roles, deputies, and contact details.
2. Define P1/P2/P3 SLA matrix with escalation timings.
3. Establish 24/7 on-call rota and test with a tabletop exercise.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: CRITICAL – 24h ACN reporting deadline legally binding; failure = fine up to 2% global turnover

gaps.Title: MG-005
gaps.field_2: NIS2 Art.23 + GDPR Art.33 / Garante per la Protezione dei Dati
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.b – GDPR Cross-Compliance
gaps.field_5: A cyber incident involving personal data (e.g., RSK-003 insider exfiltration, RSK-007 cloud breach) triggers BOTH NIS2 reporting AND GDPR Art.33 72h notification to the Garante. The framework does not document this dual-reporting workflow.
gaps.field_10:
1. Add GDPR parallel notification step to IR checklist (Phase 1 and Phase 2).
2. Define criteria for when a NIS2 incident also constitutes a personal data breach.
3. Assign DPO as mandatory member of incident war room.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – Separate €20M GDPR fine risk in addition to NIS2 penalties

gaps.Title: MG-006
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.c – OT/Trading System Continuity
gaps.field_5: BC/DR plan exists but the framework notes test results are not formally documented. For a financial market infrastructure, NIS2 + DORA require tested and documented recovery of critical trading systems (AST-001, AST-003) with RTO/RPO evidence.
gaps.field_10:
1. Conduct a full DR test of AST-001 (Trading Platform) and document results.
2. Publish RTO/RPO test results to the board as evidence of resilience.
3. Schedule biannual DR tests and link to DORA compliance evidence.
gaps.field_11: Head of Infra + Head of Trading Tech
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – Untested DR = unknown recovery capability; regulatory scrutiny during incident

gaps.Title: MG-007
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.d – CVCN 45-Day Rule
gaps.field_5: AST-002 (ION Cloud Infrastructure) has a CVCN notice issued but NOT approved. Under PSNC legislation, procuring/using strategic ICT assets without CVCN approval is a CRIMINAL OFFENCE, not just a civil fine. This is the highest-priority single gap.
gaps.field_10:
1. IMMEDIATE: Escalate ION Cloud CVCN status to ACN daily until resolved.
2. Consider interim risk-acceptance from board if approval is delayed.
3. Legal counsel to assess exposure and provide formal opinion.
4. Do not expand ION Cloud usage until CVCN approved.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: CRITICAL – Criminal liability for responsible officers; potential service suspension

gaps.Title: MG-008
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.e – Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
gaps.field_5: NIS2 Art.21.2.e explicitly requires vulnerability handling AND disclosure. LIST SpA has no published VDP. This is a visible compliance gap and also affects researcher/third-party vulnerability reporting.
gaps.field_10:
1. Draft and publish a VDP on the corporate website.
2. Designate a security contact (security@listgroup.com) for vulnerability reports.
3. Define internal SLA for triaging externally reported vulnerabilities.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: MEDIUM – Non-compliance with explicit NIS2 disclosure obligation

gaps.Title: MG-009
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.f – No Threat Hunting Programme
gaps.field_5: The framework relies on SIEM (Splunk) for detection but has no documented threat hunting programme. NIS2 Art.21.2.f requires entities to proactively assess security effectiveness. Threat hunting is industry standard for financial market infrastructure.
gaps.field_10:
1. Establish a threat hunting programme within the SOC (hypothesis-based and IOC-based).
2. Conduct quarterly threat hunts aligned to MITRE ATT&CK for Financial Services.
3. Document hunt results and feed into risk register.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – Advanced threats (APT) dwell undetected without proactive hunting

gaps.Title: MG-010
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.g – No Security Culture KPIs
gaps.field_5: Training completion is tracked informally. NIS2 requires systematic, documented, and effective cyber hygiene. Without KPIs (phishing click rates, training completion %, incident reporting rates), there is no evidence of programme effectiveness.
gaps.field_10:
1. Define security culture KPIs: phishing click rate <5%, training completion >95%.
2. Report KPIs to board quarterly.
3. Implement a security champions programme across business units.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: MEDIUM – Cannot demonstrate compliance effectiveness to ACN auditors

gaps.Title: MG-011
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2.h – Key Management Policy
gaps.field_5: AES-256 and TLS 1.3 are deployed but there is no documented key lifecycle management policy covering: key generation, distribution, storage (HSM), rotation schedule, revocation, and destruction. ACN standards require this for Strategic data.
gaps.field_10:
1. Draft Key Management Policy aligned to NIST SP 800-57 and ACN standards.
2. Implement HSM-backed key storage for all Strategic asset encryption.
3. Define key rotation schedule (e.g., annual for data at rest; immediate on compromise).
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: MEDIUM – Unmanaged key lifecycle = risk of cryptographic compromise

gaps.Title: MG-012
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.23 – No Tested ACN Notification Workflow
gaps.field_5: The IR checklist references notifying ACN via the unified digital portal (EW-04) but this has never been tested. If a real incident occurs, discovering the portal requires registration/authentication in the first frantic hours would delay notification and breach the 24h deadline.
gaps.field_10:
1. Register LIST SpA on the ACN unified portal NOW.
2. Conduct a dry-run notification test with a simulated incident.
3. Document portal credentials in a secured, accessible location for IR team.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – 24h legal deadline breach; fine up to €10M or 2% turnover

gaps.Title: MG-013
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Cooperation – No ISAC Membership
gaps.field_5: NIS2 promotes cooperation and information exchange (Art.18-19). LIST SpA has no documented membership in FS-ISAC or equivalent sector ISAC, no STIX/TAXII threat intel sharing arrangement, and no relationship documented with CSIRT Italia beyond notification.
gaps.field_10:
1. Apply for FS-ISAC membership for EU financial sector threat intelligence.
2. Establish a formal relationship with CSIRT Italia (beyond incident notification).
3. Document information sharing agreements and classify shared data.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: LOW-MEDIUM – Missed early warning intelligence; may be required by ACN for Essential entities

gaps.Title: MG-014
gaps.field_3: DORA Art.30 – ICT Contractual Arrangements
gaps.field_5: DORA Art.30 requires specific minimum contractual provisions with all ICT third-party providers (not just critical ones). The Supply Chain tab tracks vendors but does NOT track whether each contract contains the mandatory DORA clauses (audit rights, SLAs, exit plans, data location, security standards).
gaps.field_10:
1. Create a DORA contractual clause register mapping each vendor to mandatory Art.30 provisions.
2. Prioritise Critical ICT vendors (ION, Microsoft Azure, Bloomberg).
3. Schedule contract amendments during next renewal cycle for non-compliant contracts.
gaps.field_17: Risk Exposure: HIGH – DORA non-compliance for financial entity; Bank of Italy supervision

gaps.Title: ACT-001
gaps.field_3: Create 24h ACN Early Warning template and on-call escalation tree
gaps.field_10: Create 24h ACN Early Warning template and on-call escalation tree
gaps.field_17: Original | Legal deadline. Must be live before Apr 2026 enforcement.

gaps.Title: ACT-002
gaps.field_3: Board-level NIS2 cybersecurity training (management personal liability)
gaps.field_10: Board-level NIS2 cybersecurity training (management personal liability)
gaps.field_17: Original | NIS2 Art.20. Board members personally liable. OVERDUE RISK.

gaps.Title: ACT-003
gaps.field_3: IMMEDIATE: Chase daily CVCN approval for ION Cloud (AST-002) – criminal risk
gaps.field_10: IMMEDIATE: Chase daily CVCN approval for ION Cloud (AST-002) – criminal risk
gaps.field_17: NEW | D.Lgs.105/2019 PSNC – criminal offence if not approved. Escalate to CEO.

gaps.Title: ACT-004
gaps.field_3: Draft Board Cybersecurity Governance Policy & board resolution template
gaps.field_10: Draft Board Cybersecurity Governance Policy & board resolution template
gaps.field_17: NEW | Art.20 requires management to formally approve and oversee Art.21 measures.

gaps.Title: ACT-005
gaps.field_3: Register LIST SpA on ACN unified portal and conduct notification dry-run
gaps.field_10: Register LIST SpA on ACN unified portal and conduct notification dry-run
gaps.field_17: NEW | 24h deadline breach risk. Portal must be registered BEFORE an incident occurs.

gaps.Title: ACT-006
gaps.field_3: Deploy NIS2/DORA vendor questionnaire to all Critical/Important suppliers
gaps.field_10: Deploy NIS2/DORA vendor questionnaire to all Critical/Important suppliers
gaps.field_17: Original | Prioritise ION Group and Microsoft Azure. Include DORA Art.30 clauses check.

gaps.Title: ACT-007
gaps.field_3: Schedule DORA TLPT with ACN-accredited provider
gaps.field_10: Schedule DORA TLPT with ACN-accredited provider
gaps.field_17: Original | Mandatory under DORA. Select accredited Red Team provider.

gaps.Title: ACT-008
gaps.field_3: Submit CVCN 45-day notification for ION Cloud (AST-002) procurement
gaps.field_10: Submit CVCN 45-day notification for ION Cloud (AST-002) procurement
gaps.field_17: Original | Linked to ACT-011. Track to resolution.

gaps.Title: ACT-009
gaps.field_3: Update ISO 27001 Risk Assessment with NIS2 / D.Lgs.138/2024 references
gaps.field_10: Update ISO 27001 Risk Assessment with NIS2 / D.Lgs.138/2024 references
gaps.field_17: Original | Include threat intelligence framework component.

gaps.Title: ACT-010
gaps.field_3: Extend MFA to all internal SaaS tools (Zero-Trust)
gaps.field_10: Extend MFA to all internal SaaS tools (Zero-Trust)
gaps.field_17: Original | Create MFA coverage register first.

gaps.Title: ACT-011
gaps.field_3: Add GDPR parallel notification workflow to IR checklist + assign DPO to war room
gaps.field_10: Add GDPR parallel notification workflow to IR checklist + assign DPO to war room
gaps.field_17: NEW | Dual NIS2 + GDPR reporting required when personal data is involved.

gaps.Title: ACT-012
gaps.field_3: Subscribe to FS-ISAC and establish CTI programme (threat actor profiling + MITRE ATT&CK)
gaps.field_10: Subscribe to FS-ISAC and establish CTI programme (threat actor profiling + MITRE ATT&CK)
gaps.field_17: NEW | NIS2 requires systematic threat identification. FS-ISAC annual fee ~€8k.

gaps.Title: ACT-013
gaps.field_3: Create DORA Art.30 contractual clause register and remediate Critical ICT vendor contracts
gaps.field_10: Create DORA Art.30 contractual clause register and remediate Critical ICT vendor contracts
gaps.field_17: NEW | Mandatory DORA contractual provisions (audit rights, SLAs, exit plans) for ION, Azure, Bloomberg.

gaps.Title: ACT-014
gaps.field_3: Formalise SSDLC documentation with NIS2 / DORA references + add VDP
gaps.field_10: Formalise SSDLC documentation with NIS2 / DORA references + add VDP
gaps.field_17: Original | Also publish Vulnerability Disclosure Policy on corporate website.

gaps.Title: ACT-015
gaps.field_3: Cross-check cryptographic implementations against ACN approved standards
gaps.field_10: Cross-check cryptographic implementations against ACN approved standards
gaps.field_17: Original | Draft Key Management Policy (NIST SP 800-57 aligned).

gaps.Title: ACT-016
gaps.field_3: Verify all SaaS products for ACN Cloud Catalog qualification
gaps.field_10: Verify all SaaS products for ACN Cloud Catalog qualification
gaps.field_17: Original | ACN Cloud Qualification mandatory for strategic data.

gaps.Title: ACT-017
gaps.field_3: Define security KPIs (phishing <5%, training >95%) and report to board quarterly
gaps.field_10: Define security KPIs (phishing <5%, training >95%) and report to board quarterly
gaps.field_17: NEW | Without KPIs, cannot evidence training effectiveness to ACN auditors.

gaps.Title: ACT-018
gaps.field_3: Conduct formal DR test for AST-001 (Trading Platform) and document results
gaps.field_10: Conduct formal DR test for AST-001 (Trading Platform) and document results
gaps.field_17: NEW | Tested BC/DR is a NIS2 and DORA compliance requirement.

gaps.Title: ACT-019
gaps.field_3: Draft Data Classification Policy linked to NIS2 primary/support asset concept
gaps.field_10: Draft Data Classification Policy linked to NIS2 primary/support asset concept
gaps.field_17: NEW | Current tiers are informal. Formal policy needed for audit evidence.

gaps.Title: ACT-020
gaps.field_3: Establish quarterly threat hunting programme in SOC (MITRE ATT&CK aligned)
gaps.field_10: Establish quarterly threat hunting programme in SOC (MITRE ATT&CK aligned)
gaps.field_17: NEW | Required for proactive effectiveness assessment per NIS2 Art.21.2.f.

gaps.Title: MG-015
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.21.2 – Asset Inventory Completeness (OT/IoT)
gaps.field_5: OT/IoT devices and office sensors not formally inventoried or assessed under NIS2 scope. Network-connected physical infrastructure excluded from current asset register.
gaps.field_7: Asset inventory report covering OT/IoT with CIA ratings
gaps.field_9: Draft OT/IoT discovery scan; add findings to Asset Inventory; classify under NIS2 Art.21.2.k
gaps.field_10: CISO + Head of Infra
gaps.field_16: Risk Exposure: MEDIUM – Physical/OT vector overlooked; NIS2 covers broader infrastructure

gaps.Title: MG-016
gaps.field_3: DORA Art.10 – Digital Resilience Testing (Vulnerability Scanning)
gaps.field_5: Annual pen test exists but no defined frequency for infrastructure vulnerability scanning. DORA Art.10 requires regular testing beyond TLPT including vulnerability assessments.
gaps.field_7: Vulnerability scan report with remediation SLA tracking
gaps.field_9: Define monthly/quarterly vulnerability scan schedule; automate via Qualys or Nessus; report findings to CISO monthly
gaps.field_10: SOC Lead + CISO
gaps.field_16: Risk Exposure: HIGH – DORA finding; financial supervisor scrutiny if not evidenced

gaps.Title: MG-017
gaps.field_3: NIS2 Art.22 – Cyber Threat Information Sharing
gaps.field_5: No formal STIX/TAXII threat intelligence sharing agreement. No documented bilateral relationship with CSIRT Italia beyond incident notifications. NIS2 promotes proactive cooperation.
gaps.field_7: FS-ISAC membership certificate; CSIRT cooperation MOU
gaps.field_9: Apply for FS-ISAC membership; establish documented cooperation with CSIRT Italia; define classification for shared intelligence
gaps.field_10: CISO
gaps.field_16: Risk Exposure: LOW-MEDIUM – ACN may require formal cooperation framework for Essential entities

gaps.Title: ACT-021
gaps.field_17: nan | nan

risks.Title: RSK-001
risks.field_1: Ransomware on trading platform (cf. ION LockBit 2023)
risks.field_7: Immutable backups; EDR; network segmentation post-LockBit
risks.field_9: ACT-007 TLPT + Immutable backup verification (ACT-018). Deploy ZTNA per ACT-010.

risks.Title: RSK-002
risks.field_1: Supply chain compromise via 3rd-party software update
risks.field_7: CVCN vetting in progress; vendor questionnaires; change management
risks.field_9: ACT-006 vendor questionnaire + ACT-013 DORA Art.30 clauses. CVCN daily chase (ACT-003).

risks.Title: RSK-003
risks.field_7: PAM (CyberArk); quarterly access reviews; SIEM alerting
risks.field_9: Automate 24h PAM revocation on HR trigger. SIEM insider threat use-cases.

risks.Title: RSK-004
risks.field_1: DDoS attack on market data gateway
risks.field_7: DDoS scrubbing (Cloudflare); ISP filtering; redundant connectivity
risks.field_9: DDoS runbook + quarterly failover test. Cloudflare DDoS+ tier upgrade.

risks.Title: RSK-005
risks.field_9: Extend MFA to all SaaS (ACT-010). Phishing simulation quarterly with KPIs.

risks.Title: RSK-006
risks.field_1: Failure to notify ACN within 24h → regulatory sanction
risks.field_7: IR playbook; 24h on-call; ACN portal access
risks.field_9: ACN portal registration + dry-run (ACT-005). 24h EW template (ACT-001).

risks.Title: RSK-007
risks.field_7: CSPM; access control reviews; encryption at rest
risks.field_9: CSPM misconfiguration alerts + monthly Azure security score review.

risks.Title: 🆕 RSK-008
risks.field_9: IMMEDIATE: daily ACN escalation (ACT-003). Board interim risk acceptance. Legal opinion.

risks.Title: 🆕 RSK-009
risks.field_1: Board personal liability (NIS2 Art.20) – management not trained
risks.field_9: ACT-002 Board training by Mar 2026. GOV-001 board resolution. Training tracker.

risks.Title: 🆕 RSK-010
risks.field_1: DORA TLPT not conducted – financial supervisor finding
risks.field_7: Annual pen test (non-TLPT); working to schedule TLPT
risks.field_9: ACT-007 TLPT provider selection. Expand pen test scope to trading infra.

risks.Title: RSK-011
risks.field_1: GDPR-NIS2 Overlap: delayed dual-reporting triggers double regulatory penalties
risks.field_7: DPO in IR war room; GDPR Art.33 checklist added to IR playbook (MG-005)

risks.Title: RSK-012
risks.field_1: Vendor exit strategy failure – no tested exit plan for Critical ICT providers (DORA Art.30)
risks.field_7: DORA Art.30 clause register in progress (ACT-013); contractual reviews ongoing

risks.Title: RSK-013
risks.field_1: Insider threat amplified by training gaps – untrained staff create exploitable attack surface
risks.field_7: Annual awareness training (informal); phishing simulations; PAM controls

risks.Title: RSK-014
risks.field_1: Cross-border incident mismanagement – EU-wide impact not escalated per NIS2 Art.23.5
risks.field_7: IR checklist includes cross-border assessment (EW-03); DORA dual-reporting

assets.Title: AST-001
assets.field_4: Core trading engine. High-frequency order matching. Financial market critical service.

assets.Title: AST-002
assets.field_4: Azure-hosted compute for trading workloads. ION Group managed.

assets.Title: AST-003
assets.field_4: Real-time market data distribution layer. Feeds all trading desks.

assets.Title: AST-004
assets.field_4: Corporate identity provider. MFA enforced for privileged accounts.

assets.Title: AST-005
assets.field_4: SIEM feeding SOC alerting. No threat hunting playbooks documented.

assets.Title: AST-006
assets.field_4: Privileged Access Management. Session recording not confirmed for all admins.

assets.Title: AST-007
assets.field_4: Communication & collaboration. Microsoft managed. Trading desk encryption TBD.

assets.field_4: Veeam immutable backups. Air-gapped copy. Post-LockBit remediation.

incidents.Title: INC-2024-001
incidents.field_3: DDoS Attack on Market Data Gateway
incidents.field_7: Hacktivist DDoS attack – Cloudflare scrubbing activated. 2.3h outage.
incidents.field_16: Upgraded to Cloudflare DDoS+ tier. Failover tested quarterly.

incidents.Title: INC-2024-002
incidents.field_7: Criminal BEC attempt targeting CFO. MFA prevented account compromise.
incidents.field_16: Extended MFA to all SaaS platforms (ACT-010). Phishing simulation quarterly.

incidents.Title: INC-2024-003
incidents.field_7: LockBit-affiliated actor. Dev environment only. Immutable backup restored in 4h.

incidents.Title: INC-2024-004
incidents.field_7: SIEM alert – CyberArk flagged unusual PAM session. Investigation confirmed authorised activity.
incidents.field_16: PAM alerting rules tuned. No reporting required.

incidents.Title: INC-2025-001
incidents.field_3: Supply Chain Compromise Attempt – Vendor Portal
incidents.field_7: Nation-state-linked attempt via compromised vendor update channel. Blocked at CVCN checkpoint.
incidents.field_16: DORA Art.30 clauses applied to all critical ICT vendors (ACT-013).

incidents.Title: ⚠️  For 'Significant' incidents: 24h Early Warning to CSIRT Italia (cert@csirt.gov.it) AND 72h Notification AND 30-Day Final Report are all MANDATORY per NIS2 Art.23. DORA: 4h notification to Bank of Italy for ICT assets (AST-001, AST-003).

phases.Title: Phase 0
phases.field_4:
• Appoint NIS2 workgroup
  • Register with ACN (Art.27)
  • Conduct NIS2 gap assessment
  • Classify assets (Essential/Important)

phases.Title: Phase 1
phases.field_4:
• Board resolution (GOV-001)
  • Board training Art.20.2
  • Legal liability briefing
  • Cybersecurity Governance Policy

phases.Title: Phase 2
phases.field_4:
• Update risk assessment (Art.21.2.a)
  • Align DORA ICT risk framework
  • Threat intelligence feed
  • Define risk appetite statement

phases.Title: Phase 3
phases.field_4:
• 24h Early Warning template
  • 72h Full Notification template
  • On-call escalation tree
  • ACN portal test & DORA dual-notify

phases.Title: Phase 4
phases.field_4:
• CVCN vetting (PSNC assets)
  • DORA Art.30 contractual clauses
  • Vendor risk questionnaires
  • Supplier resilience testing

phases.Title: Phase 5
phases.field_4:
• MFA / ZTNA rollout (ACT-010)
  • TLPT / penetration testing
  • Immutable backup verification
  • Cryptography policy

phases.Title: Phase 6
phases.field_4:
• BIA mapping critical services
  • BC/DR test & documentation
  • Crisis communications template
  • RTO/RPO validation

phases.Title: Phase 7
phases.field_4:
• Evidence repository complete
  • External audit dry-run
  • Quarterly review cycle
  • Continuous monitoring KPIs
